publicationStmt.p: Transcribed from Bodleian Library, Lovelace Byron collection, Box 165, folios 84-85. Transcription by Lisa Gold (2022). TEI conversion for Sextant pipeline, 2026.

div.p: Dearest Mate. I asked Locock this morning about Tuesday, & he said it must depend on the result of today's drive. That result is excellent. I was less weak & tired when I returned than when I set out. I think I was out near an hour, & found the britska easy.-- The glasses do not

shut by any means close, which in case of rain would be inconvenient, but otherwise it is very good.-- Yesterday evening I was less well, owing to a very sudden & great increase of inconveniences. This seemed to make Locock a little doubtful about the journey, but today it has passed off exactly as he wished.--
I was anxious about it knowing how much my sweet mate wishes to be warbling with his bird in his own trees. But in
short I am a very nice avis, very pert & pleased with myself.--

I send you my Hen's letter of this morning, & two others which of course I have not read, though knowing from whom they come, I much wished it.-- Caroline tells Charlotte that Emily has been giving out all is wrong again at Woburn, & Hester gone off alone somewhere.--
I had a better night than usual owing to the bed here down stairs being very

good. The rooms too are most comfortable & lofty, had I hope will put you in good humour again with this house. The drawing-room has so beautiful a carpet of leopard & tiger skin mixed & excellent for wear, that I want one like it.--
I have got the pretty yellow pattern from Fryern, & much admire it, but I am afraid your prudence may think it too delicate.
My sweetest of mates, Yours most affecly The Little Bird
